myth: Antibiotics can cure colds and flu.
fact: Colds and flu are caused by viruses. Antibiotics only work against bacteria and have no effect on viral infections.

myth: If I feel better, I can stop taking my antibiotics.
fact: Stopping early can leave surviving bacteria that are more resistant, making future infections harder to treat.

myth: Leftover antibiotics from last time are fine to reuse.
fact: Leftover antibiotics may be the wrong type, wrong dose, or expired, and reusing them contributes to resistance.

myth: Stronger antibiotics are always safer and more effective.
fact: Using stronger antibiotics unnecessarily increases resistance risk and can have more side effects than needed.

myth: Antibiotic resistance only affects the person who misuses them.
fact: Resistant bacteria can spread to other people, making resistance a community and public health issue, not just personal.
